prompt: Write a TypeScript module that sends a private DM using the NIP-59 gift wrap protocol. Implement the full three-layer wrapping: (1) create an unsigned rumor event with the message content, (2) encrypt the rumor into a kind:13 seal signed by the real author, (3) encrypt the seal into a kind:1059 gift wrap signed by a random ephemeral key. Also implement the unwrapping function that a recipient would use to read the message. Use nostr-tools library. Include a complete example that wraps the message 'Secret meeting at 9pm' from Alice to Bob, then unwraps it as Bob. Save to outputs/gift-wrap.ts
expected_output: A TypeScript module implementing the full NIP-59 gift wrap protocol with three distinct layers: unsigned rumor, kind:13 seal with empty tags, and kind:1059 gift wrap with ephemeral key and p-tag. Includes both wrapping and unwrapping functions with a working example.
assertions: The rumor event is created WITHOUT a signature (no sig field) — it has an id and pubkey but is intentionally unsigned for deniability
The seal event uses kind 13 and has tags set to an empty array (tags: [])
The seal's content is the rumor JSON-stringified and encrypted with NIP-44 using the author's private key and recipient's public key
The gift wrap uses kind 1059 and includes a p-tag with the recipient's public key for routing
The gift wrap is signed by a randomly generated ephemeral key (not the author's key)
The created_at timestamps on both the seal and gift wrap are randomized (not the current time), with randomization up to 2 days in the past
The unwrapping function decrypts the gift wrap first, then verifies the seal signature, then decrypts the seal to get the rumor
The unwrapping function verifies that rumor.pubkey matches seal.pubkey (author consistency check)
